mission.title: Install and Configure Rancher / RKE2
mission.description: Rancher is a complete container management platform that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters across various environments. It is ideal for organizations looking to streamline their container orchestration and management processes. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient resources and potential complexity in multi-cluster management.

steps.title: Install the Rancher CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Rancher CLI, which is necessary for managing Rancher from the command line.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.0/rancher-darwin-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf rancher-darwin-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv rancher-darwin-amd64 /usr/local/bin/rancher

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/rancher

```

> Download the Rancher CLI for macOS.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.
> Make the CLI executable.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using RKE2 with the specified version and node count.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | sh -

sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service

sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

sudo cat /var/lib/rancher/rke2/server/node-token

```

> Install RKE2 on the server.
> Enable the RKE2 server service.
> Start the RKE2 server service.
> Retrieve the node token for joining worker nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures the kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to communicate with the new cluster.

```bash

sudo cp /etc/rancher/rke2/rke2.yaml ~/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) ~/.kube/config

```

> Copy the RKE2 kubeconfig to the user's kubeconfig.
> Change ownership of the kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the cluster nodes are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

```

> Check the status of the nodes in the cluster.
> Verify that all system pods are running.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up monitoring and autoscaling for the cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/master/manifests/rancher-monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/master/manifests/rancher-autoscaler.yaml

```

> Install monitoring for the cluster.
> Install autoscaler for the cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rancher
uninstall.description:
This step removes Rancher and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete namespace cattle-system

sudo systemctl stop rke2-server.service

sudo systemctl disable rke2-server.service

```

> Delete the Rancher namespace and all resources within it.
> Stop the RKE2 server service.
> Disable the RKE2 server service.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rancher
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step includes draining nodes if necessary, performing the upgrade, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/master/manifests/rancher-upgrade.yaml

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

```

> Check the status of nodes before upgrading.
> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Apply the upgrade manifest.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<port>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RKE2 server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the RKE2 server logs with 'sudo journalctl -u rke2-server -f' and ensure the service is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = Error response from daemon: pull access denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The image may not be available or the credentials are incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and check if authentication is required for the image repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes returns no nodes'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The nodes may not be properly joined to the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure the node token is correct and that the RKE2 agent is running on the worker nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on system pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be resource constraints or misconfigurations in the pod specifications.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource requests/limits as necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved Helm chart management, and enhanced RBAC features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy authentication methods, removed support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.9, ensure all custom resource definitions are updated to the latest versions.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'. All system pods should also be in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
